title: Add bundles pack and import commands to runex skill bundles.nu
description:
The runex skill's bundles.nu has list/show/validate but no pack or import commands. The Runex API has POST /api/bundles/import accepting multipart tar.gz uploads (BundleController.import_bundle at lib/runex_web/controllers/api/bundle_controller.ex:8). We need:

1. bundles.nu pack <bundle-dir> — creates a tar.gz archive from a bundle directory. Output to stdout path or --output flag. Uses 'tar -czf' under the hood.
2. bundles.nu import <archive-path> --host <runex-url> — POSTs the tar.gz to the target Runex's /api/bundles/import endpoint as multipart form upload. Returns the import result.

These are needed for vantageex-219 (pack VantageEx bundles and import to Mini's Runex) and for the bundle versioning epic (vantageex-218).

File: plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/scripts/0.1.0/bundles.nu
acceptance_criteria:
- bundles.nu pack creates a valid tar.gz from any bundle directory
- bundles.nu import uploads the tar.gz to a target Runex and returns the result
- Both commands have --help output
- Tested manually against localhost:4001

title: fix: /core:security hook uses relative path — check-secrets-before-commit.sh fails on every Bash call
description:
The /core:security skill's SKILL.md frontmatter defines a PreToolUse:Bash hook with a relative command path:

  command: "./hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh"

This resolves from the user's current working directory (e.g. ~/github/vantageex), not from the skill's own directory. The script only exists at the skill's hooks/ directory. Result: every Bash tool call produces 5+ non-blocking errors:

  PreToolUse:Bash hook error
  Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh: ./hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh: No such file or directory

Fix: change the command to use the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT variable (same pattern the security-guidance and hookify plugins use):

  command: "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh"

File: plugins/core/skills/security/SKILL.md (frontmatter hooks section, around line 10)
Also verify the script exists at: plugins/core/skills/security/hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh

One-line fix. Bump the plugin version in plugin.json after.
acceptance_criteria:
- Hook command uses absolute path via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT
- Loading /core:security skill no longer produces hook errors on Bash calls
- The gitleaks scan still runs correctly when the hook fires (test by running a Bash command after loading the skill)

title: fix(security): gitleaks baseline not suppressing historical findings
description: Gitleaks reports 6 findings in git history at commit SHAs 6d6e3b79, 99b860b6, e08d4800, a33118eb in paths .gitleaks-baseline.json and skills/code-review/SKILL.md. The .gitleaks-baseline.json file exists but the current gitleaks invocation does not honor it properly, so these pre-existing historical findings are not being suppressed. Findings are pre-existing on main (verified: git branch --contains 6d6e3b79 includes main). The baseline file needs to be passed correctly to the gitleaks invocation, or the fingerprints need to be added/fixed in the baseline. Fingerprints from the 6 findings should be confirmed and added to .gitleaks-baseline.json to suppress them. Do NOT modify skills/code-review/SKILL.md content unless the secrets are real and must be removed from history.

title: Fix runex.nu submit --params flag parsing
description:
runex.nu submit does not properly handle --params flag. Multiple agents in this session fell back to raw curl because the nushell flag parser intercepted --params before the command could use it. The submit command needs to accept params as a positional JSON string or fix the flag definition.

File: plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/scripts/0.1.0/runex.nu (cmd-submit function)
acceptance_criteria:
- runex.nu submit <workflow> --params '{"KEY":"val"}' works without falling back to curl
- Tested against localhost:4001

title: Fix debug.nu watch nushell parse error on interval variable
description:
debug.nu watch command fails with:

  nu::shell::external_command — Command 'interval:' not found

The string interpolation at line 153 treats 'interval:' as a command name instead of part of the print string. Nushell syntax issue.

File: plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/scripts/0.1.0/debug.nu (watch function, around line 153)
acceptance_criteria:
- debug.nu watch <run_id> polls without parse errors
- Interval parameter works

title: Add info command to runex.nu for GET /api/info
description:
The Runex API exposes GET /api/info returning git_sha, build_time, version, etc. The runex.nu skill script has no command for it. Agents had to use curl to check /api/info.

Add: runex.nu info — calls GET /api/info and returns the result as a table.

File: plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/scripts/0.1.0/runex.nu
acceptance_criteria:
- runex.nu info returns structured data with git_sha, build_time, version
- Works against any Runex host via RUNEX_HOST env

title: Update runex skill API template with bundle endpoints
description:
The API template at plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/templates/0.1.0/api.md documents GET /api/health, GET /api/workflows, GET/POST /api/runs, etc. but is missing:

- POST /api/bundles/import (multipart tar.gz upload)
- POST /api/bundles/sync (trigger bundle sync)
- POST /api/bundles/webhook
- POST /api/workflows/import (content/URL/git source)
- GET /api/info
- GET /api/runs/:id/steps/:step_id

Add request/response shapes for each based on the actual Runex router and controllers.
acceptance_criteria:
- API template covers all current Runex endpoints
- Request/response shapes match actual controller behavior
